description: Currying our way into pairing things up.

hints.text: The goal is $A \to (B \to A \land B)$. Since the outermost [connective|connective] is an [implication|implication], our first move should be to assume its antecedent, $A$.
We are currently under the [assumption|assumption] $A$. To prove the remaining [implication|implication] $B \to A \land B$, let's assume its antecedent, $B$.
Splendid. Now we have $A$ at step 1 and $B$ at step 2. Let's bundle them together into a [conjunction|conjunction] using `and-intro`.
With $A \land B$ secured at step 3, we can now discharge our most recent [assumption|assumption], $B$, using `->-intro`.
Almost there! Step 4 gives us $B \to A \land B$. Discharge our original [assumption|assumption] $A$ with one last `->-intro` to cross the finish line.
